prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='DWT_rf', seasonality_champion='ETS', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.85) and high seasonality variance (0.71), indicating models capture patterns very differently. DWT_rf excels at trend tracking (error=1.78), while ETS dominates seasonality capture (corr=0.765). THETA leads overall RMSE but horizon specialists differ (CWT_rf early, THETA late). These diverse capabilities across trend, seasonality, and horizons strongly support DBA combination to leverage complementary strengths.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "DWT_rf",
  "seasonality_champion": "ETS",
  "overall_champion": "THETA",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "CWT_rf", "late": "THETA"},
  "tier1_models": ["THETA", "ETS", "DWT_catboost", "CWT_rf", "catboost", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "DWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["CWT_svr", "rf", "FT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "DWT_svr", "CWT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.8508,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.85) and high seasonality variance (0.71), indicating models capture patterns very differently. DWT_rf excels at trend tracking (error=1.78), while ETS dominates seasonality capture (corr=0.765). THETA leads overall RMSE but horizon specialists differ (CWT_rf early, THETA late). These diverse capabilities across trend, seasonality, and horizons strongly support DBA combination to leverage complementary strengths."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25", "best_per_horizon_by_validation"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.25}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=9 indicates high horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection methods. RMSE spread between THETA (1026.6) and ONLY_FT_catboost (1976.8) is ~92%, favoring top-k over full mean. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination due to high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.85) and high seasonality variance (0.71). With n_windows=3, small top_k=3 with shrinkage=0.25 provides stability. best_per_horizon_by_validation captures horizon specialists (CWT_rf early, THETA late)."
}
raw.skeptic: {"add_names": [], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Current set has 4 candidates with diverse types: baseline (dba_combination), selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25). No leakage detected - all use safe fitting strategies. Universe leaderboard shows ridge_stacking candidates score ~0.786 which is below current best dba_combination (0.792). Current set meets diversity rules (≥2 different types, no ≥4 same-type candidates). No changes needed.", "changes": [], "when_good": ""}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.85) and high seasonality variance (0.71), indicating models capture patterns very differently. DWT_rf excels at trend tracking (error=1.78), while ETS dominates seasonality capture (corr=0.765). THETA leads overall RMSE but horizon specialists differ (CWT_rf early, THETA late). These diverse capabilities across trend, seasonality, and horizons strongly support DBA combination to leverage complementary strengths.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=9 indicates high horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection methods. RMSE spread between THETA (1026.6) and ONLY_FT_catboost (1976.8) is ~92%, favoring top-k over full mean. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination due to high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.85) and high seasonality variance (0.71). With n_windows=3, small top_k=3 with shrinkage=0.25 provides stability. best_per_horizon_by_validation captures horizon specialists (CWT_rf early, THETA late).

skeptic.rationale: Current set has 4 candidates with diverse types: baseline (dba_combination), selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25). No leakage detected - all use safe fitting strategies. Universe leaderboard shows ridge_stacking candidates score ~0.786 which is below current best dba_combination (0.792). Current set meets diversity rules (≥2 different types, no ≥4 same-type candidates). No changes needed.
